query: Ryozo Himino BOJ Deputy Governor underlying inflation accelerate above 2% target July 2026 statement

mako.raw:
{"query":"Ryozo Himino BOJ Deputy Governor underlying inflation accelerate above 2% target July 2026 statement","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1721,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.boj.or.jp/en/mopo/diet/d_state/dst260619a.htm","title":"Statement by Deputy Governor HIMINO concerning the Bank's Semiannual Report on Currency and Monetary Control (Committee on Financial Affairs, House of Representatives) : 日本銀行 Bank of Japan","content":"Japan's economy has recovered moderately, although some weakness has been seen in part, partly due to the impact of the situation in the Middle East.
With regard to the outlook, while higher crude oil prices are likely to exert downward pressure on economic activity, Japan's economy is expected to be supported by factors such as high levels of corporate profits.
On the price front, the year-on-year rate of increase in the consumer price index (CPI) for all items excluding fresh food has recently been at around 1.5 percent, due to factors such as the effects of the government's measures to reduce the household burden of higher energy prices.
However, the rate of increase is likely to accelerate to a level clearly above 2 percent, as the rise in crude oil prices is expected to push up prices, mainly of energy and goods.
Meanwhile, underlying CPI inflation is expected to increase gradually, coming to a level that is generally consistent with the price stability target between the second half of fiscal 2026 and fiscal 2027.
There are various risks to the outlook.
On the price front, there appears to be a risk that price increases stemming from high crude oil prices will spread across a wide range of items, as well as a risk that underlying CPI inflation will deviate upward to a level above the price stability target of 2 percent.
As a result, the Bank decided that, through March 2027, it would maintain the existing reduction plan decided in June 2025 and that, from April 2027, it would keep the amount of its monthly JGB purchases at about 2 trillion yen."},{"url":"https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-06-19/boj-s-himino-notes-risk-of-price-trend-rising-above-2-target","title":"Japan Inflation Risk May Surpass 2% Target, BOJ’s Himino Says - Bloomberg","content":"Bank of Japan Deputy Governor Ryozo Himino said he sees a risk that inflation could accelerate beyond the central bank’s 2% target, explaining to government officials the rationale for raising interest rates earlier this week.
“We are seeing the pass-through of higher costs into prices in business-to-business transactions proceed at a somewhat faster pace, and we believe this could eventually spread to broader price increases across a wide range of consumer goods and services,” Himino said in response to questions in parliament Friday."}]}
